conditions.description: Endoscopy must be conducted within 35 days before randomisation
If no diagnostic colonoscopy with serial biopsy has been performed within one year of screening, a full colonoscopy is required
conditions.applies_if: no diagnostic colonoscopy with serial biopsy within one year of screening

conditions.description: Including mucosal biopsies

footnotes.raw_text: Including mucosal biopsies. During screening, diagnostic colonoscopy or flexible sigmoidoscopy, at the discretion of the Investigator (if no diagnostic colonoscopy with serial biopsy has been performed within one year of screening, a full colonoscopy is required, to exclude malignancy), centrally read. Endoscopy conducted within 35 days before Randomisation is acceptable. At Visit 8, flexible sigmoidoscopy, centrally read.
footnotes.notes: Assumed that 'centrally read' applies to both screening endoscopy and Visit 8 flexible sigmoidoscopy. The specific timing requirement for Visit 8 flexible sigmoidoscopy is not explicitly stated.

conditions.description: screening endoscopy should be performed between Day -35 and Day -6 prior to randomisation

footnotes.raw_text: The screening endoscopy should be performed at Day -35 to Day -6 prior to Randomisation.
footnotes.notes: Timing window for screening endoscopy is clearly specified but no specific procedural details provided

footnotes.procedure: Stool frequency and rectal bleeding scoring

conditions.description: Scores calculated as average based on scores collected from Patient daily Diary
Collection period is up to 5 days but at least 3 days prior to each applicable visit
Stool frequency and rectal bleeding subscore considered missing if patient undergoes bowel preparation for endoscopy
Stool frequency and rectal bleeding subscore considered missing for day of all endoscopies
Stool frequency and rectal bleeding subscore considered missing for day after all endoscopies

key_fields.procedure_type: Stool frequency and rectal bleeding scoring

footnotes.raw_text: The scores for stool frequency and rectal bleeding will be calculated as an average based on scores collected from the Patient daily Diary, for up to 5, but at least 3 days prior to each applicable visit. If the patient undergoes bowel preparation for endoscopy any of the days before a visit, the stool frequency and rectal bleeding subscore for that day(s) should be considered missing. In addition, the stool frequency and rectal bleeding subscore will be considered missing for the day of all endoscopies and the day after.
footnotes.notes: Assumed 'applicable visit' refers to study visits where this scoring is required. No specific endoscopy details provided.

footnotes.procedure: Blood sample collection for PK assessment

conditions.description: Mainland China PK subgroup only
Blood samples collected at specific time points relative to drug administration
Blood samples collected at specific time points relative to drug administration
Blood samples collected at specific time points relative to drug administration

key_fields.procedure: Blood sample collection for PK assessment

footnotes.raw_text: For Mainland China PK subgroup only. At 1st dose: pre-dose and 0.5, 1, 2 (end of infusion), 6, 48, 144 (Day 6), and 240 (Day 10) h after the start of the 1st administration; At the 2nd, 3rd , 4th, and 5th administrations blood samples will be collected pre-dose and at the end of infusion; At 6th dose: pre-dose and 0.5, 1, 2 (end of infusion), 6, 48, 144 (Day 76), 240 (Day 80), 336 (Day 84), 480 (Day 90), and 840 h (Day 105) after the start of the 6th administration.
footnotes.notes: Assumed 'end of infusion' corresponds to 2 hours based on explicit mention at 2h. Day numbers in parentheses were interpreted as additional timepoint context rather than separate values.

footnotes.procedure: Biomarker analysis including ESR, CRP, neutrophil and platelet count (local site); IL-6, IL-6/sIL-6R complex, faecal calprotectin (central lab)

conditions.description: Patient should be in fasting state
Local site analysis
conditions.value: ESR, CRP, neutrophil and platelet count

conditions.description: Central lab analysis

key_fields.analytes: ESR, CRP, neutrophil count, platelet count, IL-6, IL-6/sIL-6R complex, faecal calprotectin

footnotes.raw_text: Biomarkers erythrocyte sedimentation rate [ESR], C-reactive protein (CRP), neutrophil and platelet count tests will be be analysed in local sites. IL-6, IL-6/sIL-6R complex, and faecal calprotectin tests will be analysed in central lab. At Visits 2 and 8, the patient should be in a fasting state (≥8 hours).
footnotes.notes: Collection weeks and storage temperature not specified. Assumed that all biomarkers are collected at same timepoints unless otherwise indicated.

footnotes.notes: Timing window is clearly specified as -5 to -1 days relative to randomisation
